questions.text: Is reducing data movement a priority?
Do you want to minimize synchronizations even if it sacrifices some balance?
Do you want to preserve locality even if it sacrifices aggressive balancing?
Would you like an extra refinement step (a local search algorithm)?
Should the scheduler coarsen the DAG before scheduling?
Do you need an optimal (ILP-based) solution?

schedulers.label: Serial
schedulers.desc: Schedules all tasks serially on the same processor.

schedulers.label: BspLocking
schedulers.desc: Barrier list scheduler; focuses on fewer synchronization barriers.

schedulers.label: Variance
schedulers.desc: Barrier list scheduler; focuses on workload balance and uses static priorities.

schedulers.label: GreedyBsp
schedulers.desc: Barrier list scheduler; reduces communication volume besides work balance.

schedulers.label: GrowLocal
schedulers.desc: Scheduling heuristic that focuses on work balance and preserves inherent locality.

schedulers.label: GrowLocalKL
schedulers.desc: GrowLocal initialization with Kernighan-Lin local search improvement.

schedulers.label: LockingKL
schedulers.desc: BspLocking initialization with Kernighan-Lin local search improvement.

schedulers.label: GreedyBspHC
schedulers.desc: Initialization with GreedyBsp, then improvement by Hill Climbing local search.

schedulers.label: Etf
schedulers.desc: The classic Earliest Time First scheduler.

schedulers.label: FullILP
schedulers.desc: Optimal BSP schedule via ILP (COPT), minimizing work, communication, and synchronization costs.
